channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US–Iran tensions escalate amid continued strikes and stalled ceasefire negotiations
item.description: The United States and Iran have exchanged military strikes over the weekend as diplomatic efforts to finalize a ceasefire agreement continue to stall. President Donald Trump has requested revisions to...

item.title: CBSE opens Class 12 answer sheet verification and re-evaluation portal at cbse.gov.in; application process begins
item.description: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has opened the Class 12 re-evaluation and verification portal on its official website cbse.gov.in from June 1, 2026. This facility allows students who a...

item.title: DCI arrests drug trafficking suspect carrying eight bags of bhang in Bomet operation
item.description: Detectives from the Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) in Bomet County have arrested a man suspected of drug trafficking following an intelligence-led operation along the Silibwet–Bomet Road...

item.title: Ditsong National Museum opens Regiments Room showcasing Johannesburg’s reserve force military heritage
item.description: A new exhibition space known as the Regiments Room has opened at the Ditsong National Museum of Military History in Saxonwold, Johannesburg, celebrating the city’s long-forgotten reserve force regimen...

item.title: EU Foreign Affairs Chief Kaja Kallas visits Pakistan for 8th Strategic Dialogue
item.description: European Union’s High Representative for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy, Kaja Kallas, arrived in Pakistan on Monday to participate in the 8th EU-Pakistan Strategic Dialogue. She will co-chair the...

item.title: AIU probes irregularities in recognition of university athletes
item.description: The Association of Indian Universities (AIU) is currently under scrutiny not only for alleged financial and administrative irregularities but also for serious issues regarding the certification of uni...

item.title: Middle Belt Tigers emerge inaugural champions of Nigeria Super League
item.description: The Middle Belt Tigers don make history as dem clinch the first-ever Nigeria Super League title after a thrilling six-run win against Southern Sharks at the TBS Cricket Oval in Lagos. Dem posted 104/5...

item.title: Scientists Investigate a Reversal in a Large Flow Region of Earth's Outer Core Detected in 2010
item.description: Researchers analyzing satellite measurements of Earth's magnetic field have identified an unusual event that occurred in 2010 within Earth's outer core. According to the study, a large region of molte...

item.title: Centre approves appointment of five Supreme Court judges following Collegium recommendation
item.description: The Central Government has approved the appointment of five new judges to the Supreme Court of India, significantly increasing the court’s working strength. Law Minister Arjun Ram Meghwal announced th...

item.title: Vietnamese President To Lam’s Scheduled Senate Visit and Meeting with Senate President Cayetano Called Off During Manila State Visit
item.description: Vietnam canceled the scheduled visit of President To Lam to the Philippine Senate as well as his planned meeting with Senate President Alan Peter Cayetano on June 1, 2026. According to the Senate’s Of...

item.title: US and Iran exchange new military strikes as ceasefire talks continue and Israel advances in southern Lebanon
item.description: The article reports on escalating tensions between the United States and Iran despite ongoing efforts to preserve a fragile ceasefire and reopen the Strait of Hormuz to international shipping. Iran’s ...

item.title: Supreme Court rulings could reshape U.S. midterm elections through voting and campaign finance cases
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court is playing a significant role in shaping the political landscape ahead of the upcoming midterm elections, with several major rulings and pending cases that could affect voting r...

item.title: Brazilian Socialite Roseli Vieira Dies Shortly After Cosmetic Procedure
item.description: Roseli Fernandes Vieira, a well-known socialite and influencer from Brazil, tragically passed away at the age of 48 on May 26, just a day after undergoing a cosmetic buttock enhancement procedure. Rep...

item.title: NDLEA intercepts military-grade ammunition and large drug consignments in Kaduna and other states
item.description: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) has recorded multiple major interceptions across Kaduna and several other states, including the seizure of 380 rounds of military-grade ammunition and ...

item.title: Sri Lankan Tamil Refugee Teen Killed in Chennai After Dispute Escalates Into Fatal Road Rage Incident
item.description: An 18-year-old Sri Lankan Tamil refugee named Yansi lost her life in a fatal road rage incident in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, during the early hours of May 30. According to police, the incident stemmed from...

item.title: 2027 elections: The dangerous ease of candidate imposition dressed as consensus
item.description: The Premium Times editorial scrutinizes the 2027 party primaries across Nigeria, highlighting how some governors and party leaders have manipulated the nomination process under the guise of consensus....

item.title: Solar-Powered Desalination Produces Drinking Water and Recovers Valuable Minerals
item.description: Researchers at the University of Rochester, led by Professor Chunlei Guo, have developed an innovative solar-powered desalination system that converts seawater into drinking water while recovering nea...

item.title: Middle-Belt Tigers Defeat Southern Sharks by Six Runs to Claim First Nigeria Super League Cricket Title
item.description: Middle-Belt Tigers emerged champions of the maiden Nigeria Super League cricket tournament after beating Southern Sharks by six runs in a closely contested final held at the TBS Oval in Lagos. The vic...

item.title: Shubham Kumar secures AIR 1 in JEE Advanced 2026 with 330/360 marks; over 56,800 candidates qualify
item.description: The Indian Institutes of Technology (IIT) entrance examination, JEE Advanced 2026, has announced its results with Shubham Kumar from the IIT Delhi zone securing All India Rank (AIR) 1. He achieved an ...

item.title: UK MPs to Debate NHS Single Patient Record Aiming to Improve Care Coordination
item.description: Plans to introduce a single patient record across the NHS in England will be discussed in Parliament for the first time on Monday. The system aims to integrate GP, hospital, and social care data, allo...
